theme: What happens 72 hours before 200 people land in a foreign city

linkedin.text:
72 hours before 200 people arrive. Here's what our team is doing.

Most people see the gala dinner. They don't see the 72 hours before it.

T-72h — Our on-site coordinator does a full venue walkthrough. Every table, every AV cable, every emergency exit. Printed checklist: 140 items.

T-48h — Hotel room allocation confirmed. Every VIP gets their preference. Dietary restrictions cross-checked with catering. Allergen card on every relevant plate.

T-36h — Airport ops briefed. Driver assignments, name boards, backup vehicles confirmed. WhatsApp group created for real-time updates.

T-24h — Technical rehearsal. Full audio check, slide deck loaded, lighting programmed. We find 7 things to fix. We fix them.

T-12h — Team dinner. Every team member reviews their role for the next 48h. Questions answered. Contingency protocols reviewed.

T-0 — First guests land. Welcome team in position. The event has already been run perfectly — in our heads — three times.

This is what "nothing goes wrong" looks like. It's not luck.

What surprised you most about what happens behind the scenes of a major event?

#EventProduction #BehindTheScenes #CorporateEvents #EventPlanning #EventProfessionals #UproductionEvents #MICE #IncentiveTravel #EventOps #TeamBuilding
